varieties.name: Native Australian Jute 🏆
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Brisbane area, southeastern Queensland and northern NSW, pre-1788
varieties.traits: Short-lived perennial (3-4 years), yellow 5-petal flowers, reaches 1-2m height, acute leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than commercial varieties, not cultivated for commercial fiber production
varieties.availability: Not commercially available - conservation plantings only through environmental restoration programs

varieties.name: White Jute (Species)
varieties.origin: Indo-Myanmar region and South China, cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Annual, erect shrub to 2-4m, acute leaves, yellow flowers, globular fruits

varieties.name: Tossa Jute (Species)
varieties.origin: Native to South Asia and Africa, cultivated for millennia
varieties.traits: Annual herbaceous, 1.5-4m height, serrate acute leaves 6-10cm long, yellow flowers
varieties.yield_notes: Good, 100-130 days maturity depending on variety
varieties.availability: Seeds available internationally, sold as Molokhia/Egyptian Spinach/Jew's Mallow for culinary use. Boondie Seeds Australia stocks seeds.

varieties.name: Traditional Tossa Jute (Paat) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional cultivation in Kerala since 19th century, Bengal origin
varieties.traits: Traditional tossa type, 3-4m height, golden fiber, adapted to Kerala monsoon
varieties.yield_notes: Good under monsoon conditions, 4-6 months maturity

varieties.name: O-9897 (Falguni Tossa) 🏆
varieties.traits: Tall cylindrical green stem, 3.00-3.25m height, ovate lanceolate leaves
varieties.yield_notes: High - 2.50-3.40 t/ha, excellent seed production 557 kg/ha. 120-130 days flowering.
varieties.growing_notes: Moderate pest tolerance, lower disease incidence
varieties.availability: Widely available through Indian agricultural supply chains, ICAR-CRIJAF, state agriculture departments

varieties.name: BJRI Tossa Pat 4 (O-72)
varieties.traits: Tall unbranched cylindrical green stem, less photosensitive than older varieties
varieties.yield_notes: 2.5-3.2 t/ha, suitable for 3-crop pattern. 100 days harvest.
varieties.availability: Available through ICAR network, Kerala agricultural universities

varieties.name: BJRI Tossa Pat 5 (O-795/Lal Tossa)
varieties.traits: DISTINCTIVE red/reddish stem color, tall 3.5-4.0m, quick growing, red stipule, light reddish petiole. First red-pigmented tossa variety.
varieties.yield_notes: High - 2.70-3.20 t/ha (10% higher than O-72). 110 days harvest, 140-155 days flowering.
varieties.availability: ICAR-CRIJAF, state agricultural departments, Kerala Agricultural University networks

varieties.name: BJRI Tossa Pat 6 (O-3820)
varieties.yield_notes: Good fiber and seed yield. Best seed quality: 79.67% normal seedlings, 10.33% abnormal seedlings.

varieties.name: OM-1
varieties.traits: Tall cylindrical deep green stem, ovate lanceolate leaves, length-breadth ratio 2.5:1, long cylindrical indehiscent fruit, brownish seeds. 3.00-3.25m height.

varieties.name: O-4 🏆
varieties.origin: Historical variety, Bangladesh/India, established variety pre-1980s
varieties.traits: Traditional tossa type, photosensitive for late sowing
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate, used as parent variety for breeding programs

varieties.name: JRO-524 (Baisakhi Tossa) 🏆
varieties.origin: ICAR-Central Research Institute for Jute and Allied Fibres (CRIJAF), India, 1950s-1970s era
varieties.traits: Early maturing, high-yielding variety developed for Indian conditions
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential for Indian agro-climatic zones

varieties.name: JRO-632 (Baisakhi Tossa) 🏆
varieties.traits: High-yielding, developed during critical period to increase Indian jute productivity
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, instrumental in doubling jute productivity from 1.50 to 2.04 t/ha

varieties.name: JRO-878
varieties.availability: ICAR-CRIJAF, Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, state departments

varieties.name: JRO-204 (Suren)
varieties.availability: Widely available - ICAR-CRIJAF, Kerala Agricultural University, state agricultural departments

varieties.name: JRO-66 (Golden Jubilee Tossa) 🏆
varieties.traits: Special variety released during Golden Jubilee celebrations

varieties.name: CO-58 (Sourav)
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University/Coimbatore research station, India
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield for South Indian climate

varieties.name: JBO-2003H (Ira)
varieties.availability: Research institutes, agricultural universities (note: hybrid seeds need repurchasing annually)

varieties.name: AAUOJ-1 (Tarun)
varieties.yield_notes: Good yield for high rainfall regions

varieties.name: Traditional White Jute (Deshi Paat) 🏆
varieties.origin: Indo-Myanmar region, cultivated in India for centuries

varieties.name: JRC-321 (Navin) 🏆
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, instrumental in increasing Indian jute productivity

varieties.name: JRC-7447
varieties.availability: ICAR-CRIJAF, Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, state agricultural departments

varieties.name: CVL-1 🏆
varieties.traits: Widely cultivated white jute variety, standard check variety in trials
varieties.yield_notes: Good, used as control in breeding trials

varieties.name: BJRI Deshi Pat Series (5, 6, 7, 8, 9)
varieties.traits: Progressive improvements in yield, disease resistance, and adaptation. Photo-insensitive traits in some varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, progressive improvements across series

varieties.name: JRC-698
varieties.traits: Widely cultivated, check variety in breeding programs
